headline: Gig Workers Act 2025: Mandatory SOCSO Registration; EPF Retirement Still Outside
context: The Gig Workers Act 2025 (Act 872) makes platform-provider registration and PERKESO self-employed contributions mandatory. EPF retirement contributions are not included. Accident-related disability is covered; ordinary paid sick leave is not created by the Act.

cards.big: Act 872 makes platform-provider registration and PERKESO contribution mandatory for gig workers; EPF retirement is not included.
cards.sub: Accident-related temporary disability is covered through PERKESO self-employed. EPF retirement contributions and ordinary paid sick leave remain outside the Act.

cards.big: Voluntary opt-in retirement schemes have shown limited reach. EPF i-Saraan had 291,743 participants in 2022.
cards.sub: DOSM 2022: 2.88 million own-account workers + 489,800 unpaid family workers. World Bank notes that i-Saraan take-up remained low despite increased outreach.

cards.big: Employment Act section 101C creates a rebuttable presumption of employee/employer status. It does not exclude app-based engagement from the definition.
cards.sub: Platform companies' legal status under contract-of-service tests remains a live question; section 101C shifts who bears the rebuttal burden.

cards.big: Accident SOCSO is mandatory under Act 872. Retirement and ordinary sick-leave income smoothing remain outside the Act.
cards.sub: PERKESO self-employed coverage includes accident-related temporary disability income. The Act does not create general paid sick leave or EPF contributions.

cards.big: Accident-related coverage closes the cheapest gap. Retirement income and ordinary illness income smoothing remain unaddressed.
Like fitting seatbelts but not building roads. The protection is real, the missing infrastructure is bigger.
cards.sub: Workplace accidents are the easiest risk to insure. Long-tail risks like outliving savings are the hardest, and the framework does not touch them.

cards.big: Act 872 is Malaysia's first dedicated gig-worker statute and a real adaptation. Section 7 allows further terms to be negotiated; the retirement gap remains open.
